query: We have 3 testimonials on our homepage. One is from 'John D.' with no company, one is a made-up metric ('10x ROI'), and one is from a real named customer with a verified case study.
expected_behavior: Flags 'John D.' without company attribution as a trust risk that increases Anxiety friction
Flags '10x ROI' without a source or methodology as an unsupported claim that must be removed or cited
Validates the third testimonial as acceptable social proof
Explains why fabricated social proof hurts conversion (savvy users detect it, trust is destroyed)
Does not proceed to optimize conversion elements until the trust risks are addressed
